overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, totaling 3580 followers. Content primarily focuses on research, publications, job opportunities, and conference participation in the field of computational social science and disinformation studies. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn showing higher interaction rates.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research findings, new publications, and methodological discussions.
Announcing job openings and research opportunities within the team.
Promoting conference appearances and related topics.
Content specifically related to coordinated inauthentic behavior and disinformation detection.

recommendations: Diversify content formats to include images and videos to increase engagement.
Tailor content to each platform's audience for maximum impact.
Encourage more interaction by posing questions and prompting discussion.
Increase posting frequency on platforms with lower engagement rates.
Use relevant hashtags to expand reach and connect with a broader audience.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows strong engagement with research announcements and PhD opportunities, indicating an audience interested in academic content. Reposts are a significant driver of engagement on this platform.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon content receives moderate engagement, potentially due to the smaller follower base. Focus on sharing research updates and engaging in relevant discussions within the Mastodon community.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn demonstrates the highest engagement, particularly with announcements of grants and job opportunities. Leverage this platform for professional networking and career-related content.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
